howItWorks.intro: This ai data labeling cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Total Labeling Cost
rules.formula: Total Cost = Number of Samples x Cost per Sample
rules.explanation: Multiply your dataset size by the per-sample rate based on task complexity and labeling method.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.q: What is the cheapest way to label training data?
faq.a: AI-assisted labeling with human review is cheapest and fastest, using a pre-trained model to generate initial labels that humans then correct.

faq.q: How much labeled data do I need for a good ML model?
faq.a: It varies widely: text classifiers can work with 1,000-5,000 samples, while image segmentation models often need 10,000+ labeled examples.

howWeCalculate.intro: This ai data labeling cost calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
